component.name: Hyprcorrect
component.summary: Keyboard-driven desktop spelling and typo correction

description.p: Hyprcorrect is a keyboard-driven spelling and typo corrector for the whole desktop. Press a hotkey and the word or sentence you just typed is checked and fixed in place, in any focused app including terminals.

Pluggable providers — Spellbook (offline, bundled), LanguageTool (self- hosted), and an LLM (Claude / OpenAI) — let you choose between instant offline corrections and context-aware fixes for homonyms and grammar. A privacy blocklist keeps password fields and other sensitive apps untouched.

ul.li: kinetic rubber-band scroll in prefs
auto-relaunch on Accessibility grant
prefs Dock, native scroll, Docker UX
ship the rubber-band scroll in the prebuild
add macOS platform backend
harden correction emit + review popup UX
mouse wheel no longer triggers kinetic over-scroll
update Cargo.lock dependencies
enable vsync to cap the kinetic-scroll render rate
update Cargo.lock dependencies
bump kanso to 0.1.3 (over-scroll fixes + final tune)
update Cargo.lock dependencies
bump kanso to 0.1.2 (fixes the prefs scroll frame-rate leak)
update Cargo.lock dependencies
bump kanso to 0.1.1 for the macOS-tuned scroll feel
rustfmt the prefs footer overlay
depend on published kanso 0.1.0 instead of the path dep
reset section scroll to top on navigation
rework the action footer — "Quit" + stale-daemon relaunch overlay
pad the content bottom to match the sides
kinetic scroll momentum
kinetic scroll_view + searchable app-picker combo
route running-app blocklist picker through kanso app_picker
route editable_combo through kanso
route info_icon through kanso::widgets::info_icon
bring the review popup under the kanso theme
adopt kanso widgets + decouple font install
styles+scrollbar, font dedup, dirty footer
use kanso's control treatment for inputs/buttons
delegate text/nav helpers to the design system
gate the daemon review-llm flow to Linux
wire all LLM backends + LanguageTool fallback toggle
key glyphs, per-command vim help, open prefs on Ask LLM
vim-start review option; tiling; n-gram + UI polish
multi-provider LLM config with tabs, per-backend keys
escalate to the LLM on demand + LanguageTool n-gram install
word-diff column alignment, adaptive width, wrap line-height
responsive popup, revert-to-original, suggestion list
gather ranked per-word backup suggestions
per-option word definitions (offline default, online opt-in)
one-click "Download n-grams" button
request the picky rule level
type newlines as Shift+Enter so multi-line edits don't submit
remove the "Clear" links from the Hotkeys page
Add word definition line
cap floating window width at 900 via compositor resize
editable combo opens below the field; styling pass
show app-downloaded n-grams (read-only) with a Remove option
track n-grams separately from the LanguageTool container
vim spell-suggest (z=), multi-line fix, Esc, field wrap
column-align the Original in vim mode too
picking a suggestion advances to the next correction
undo, redo, and repeat
align each correction directly under the word it replaces
Home and End keys
backup-suggestion dropdown under the focused field
virtualedit-style vertical navigation
squiggle underlines + borderless auto-sized fields
inline word-edit and Ctrl+E vim modes in the review popup
hide "Ask LLM" when the correction is already the LLM's
code-fence class-name caption; drop dead maxsize rule
tile by default; cap floating width; persist n-gram folder
vernier-style code pills; dropdown width; portal float
record chord modifiers in canonical CTRL+SHIFT+ALT+SUPER order
widen blocklist app picker so its Add button aligns
correct vim caret/squiggle anchoring under the 1.5× line-height
match vim mode's vertical spacing + line-height to word mode
keep the vim caret tight against a word after cw
render "Reload n-grams" as a framed button
hard-cap floating width via runtime max_size (no overshoot)
uniform 30px controls, fixed float, 900px cap, privacy polish
equal control heights, balanced padding, Save on key row
non-focused border on inputs; right-align Save provider
dropdown width, flush scrollbar, combo height, bar padding
stop combos overflowing the right margin; n-gram Browse + details
cap form column width so inputs don't clip on wide windows
correct LanguageTool URL label — it's the base URL
size the popup wide enough to keep the sentence on one line
fold trailing punctuation into a word's column
drop the floating-width cap entirely
tidy Providers page with info icons + framed Remove
clarify the "Reload n-grams" tooltip
move the n-gram folder field below the n-grams controls
roomier action buttons with a filled primary
add install-desktop subcommand for cargo-install users
gate install-desktop to Linux so macOS builds
install desktop integration once, not every daemon start
bind fix_sentence and review by default
make SVG square so AppStream/Flatpak accept it
